verses.commentary: At that time. Judah left brothers. With Hirah.
Married her. Like Esau's marriages (26:34).
She became pregnant and gave birth to a son, who was named Er. First son.
She conceived again and gave birth to a son and named him Onan. Second son. Named Onan.
Third son. At Kezib. Three sons listed.
Her name Tamar. Arranged marriage.
Er was wicked. LORD put him to death. Divine judgment. Swift death.
Levirate marriage law (Deuteronomy 25:5-6).
Selfish refusal.
Divine judgment strikes again. The pattern continues: Er dies, now Onan. God sees the heart.
Judah fears. Blames Tamar. Promise implied but not sincere.
After long time. Judah's wife died. Sheep shearing. With Hirah. Time passes. Judah moves on.
To shear sheep.
Covered with veil. Tamar takes action. Judah broke his promise.
Face covered. Assumes she's a cult prostitute (common at sheep shearing festivals).
Judah solicits what he thinks is a prostitute. Tamar the widow negotiates with her father-in-law. Irony piled on irony.
Tamar demands insurance. She needs proof this happened.
The pledge items are identity markers: seal (signature), cord, and staff (authority). Judah hands over his very identity.
She left. Evidence secured.
Didn't find her. Judah tries to fulfill promise and retrieve evidence. Too late.
She's vanished.
Didn't find her.
After all, I did send her this young goat, but you didn't find her. Cut losses.
Tamar is pregnant. Harsh penalty.
Being brought out. Public exposure.
Acknowledges her righteousness and his failure.
Twin boys. Twins again in this family line.
As she was giving birth, one of them put out his hand; so the midwife took a scarlet thread and tied it on his wrist and said, This one came out first.
Drew back hand. How you have broken out! Named Perez (פֶּרֶץ), breaking out.
Perez becomes ancestor of David and Jesus (Matthew 1:3). God works through unlikely circumstances.
